summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (MSC3861) for Moltis, enabling users on modern homeservers like matrix.org (which uses Matrix Authentication Service) to connect their accounts without password auth.
summary.outcome: Full OIDC authentication flow implemented and working end-to-end: two-phase browser flow (start/complete), session persistence, token refresh, moltis_owned cross-signing bootstrap via OAuth, and UI integration across channels page and onboarding wizard. Multiple iterative bug fixes required to reach a functional state.

repo: MatrixPlugin methods exposed to the gateway must be in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin`, not in `impl MatrixPlugin` — the gateway dispatches through `dyn ChannelPlugin` so inherent methods are invisible.
Channel status uses `ch.status === 'connected'` not `ch.connected` — the status field is a string enum.
RPC responses use `res.payload` for data, not `res.result`.
The OAuth callback route is `/auth/callback`, not `/api/oauth/callback`.
matrix_auth().logged_in() returns false for OIDC sessions; client.user_id().is_some() works for both auth methods.
MAS (matrix.org) rejects HTTPS localhost redirect URIs — requires http://localhost per RFC 8252 loopback native app spec.
MAS requires client_uri to be a real HTTPS project URL, not localhost — it's a metadata field for the consent screen, not a server address.
Ownership bootstrap for OIDC: bootstrap_cross_signing_if_needed(None) no-ops if cross-signing exists from a prior session; must check if this device has signing keys and call reset_identity() if not.

reset_identity() requires browser approval from MAS (CrossSigningResetAuthType::OAuth) when prior cross-signing exists — generates an approval URL at account.matrix.org/account/?action=org.matrix.cross_signing_reset.
The config passed to oidc_start from the gateway was hardcoded and discarded all form fields (ownership_mode, dm_policy, etc.) — needed to pass full config JSON through.
OIDC channel shows as connected (user_id().is_some()) before sync and ownership bootstrap complete — JS polling detects connected too early, missing ownership errors.

code.finding: Status probe used matrix_auth().logged_in() which is false for OIDC; changed to client.user_id().is_some()
normalize_redirect_uri() rewrites https://localhost to http://localhost for MAS compliance; also client_uri must be project URL not server address
oidc_start/oidc_complete must be trait methods with default noop impls — gateway calls through dyn ChannelPlugin
Original oauth_start built hardcoded config ignoring all form fields; fixed to pass full config JSON from params
ensure_oidc_owned_encryption_state: bootstrap may need reset_identity() + retry loop for signing key propagation; handles CrossSigningResetAuthType::OAuth approval flow

workflow: Preact/HTM VDOM reuse causes ghost text when button text changes simultaneously with disabled state — wrapping button in key=${state} forces DOM node replacement instead of text node patching.
Safari specifically doesn't repaint button text nodes correctly during opacity transitions — the old painted pixels persist composited behind new text.
matrix-sdk's store module re-exports RoomLoadSettings as matrix_sdk::store::RoomLoadSettings.
Localized::new requires explicit type annotation for empty Vec: Vec::<(LanguageTag, String)>::new()
Greptile code review caught: Debug derive exposing tokens, blocking std::fs in async, URL parameter injection, polling interval leak, panic in production code.
Light-on-light color patterns (text-emerald-100 on bg-emerald-500/10, text-sky-100 on bg-sky-500/10, text-amber-50 on bg-amber-500/10) were systematically broken across all ownership/status banners.

friction: Multiple round-trips fixing MAS registration errors (invalid_redirect_uri, invalid_client_metadata) because there were no integration tests against a real MAS instance.
Wrong callback path /api/oauth/callback vs /auth/callback only discovered at runtime.
https:// vs http:// loopback normalization was added, removed, and re-added across three commits due to lack of end-to-end testing.
Preact ghost button issue took 8+ attempts to diagnose and fix — required understanding Safari compositing behavior.
Ownership bootstrap timing issue (OIDC marks connected before sync/ownership completes) required a 2-second delay workaround rather than a proper async signal.
After Moltis restart, in-memory identity reset handles are lost so retry_ownership failed with 'unknown channel account' error.
m.secret_storage.default_key deserialization warnings appear on every sync — pre-existing matrix-sdk issue creating noise in logs.
open_items: OIDC channels are not properly restored from the store on Moltis restart — sessions need to survive process restarts.
The 2-second delay before closing the OIDC waiting modal is a timing hack — should properly await the ownership bootstrap completion signal.
MAS requires browser approval for cross-signing reset on every new device — UX could be improved with clearer upfront guidance before starting OIDC flow.
Duplicate Matrix account guard added for password/token in start_account but not tested for edge cases (e.g. same user reconnecting after removal).
E2E Playwright tests in channels-matrix.spec.js test UI structure but cannot test the actual OIDC flow against a real MAS instance.
oidc_issuer field was removed as dead config — may need to be re-added when matrix-sdk exposes issuer override API.
The pending_identity_reset handle is in-memory only — if the process restarts between 'Open approval page' and 'Retry', users get a confusing error requiring another reset cycle.
Recovery is consistently 'disabled' after OIDC ownership bootstrap — secret storage setup is not being triggered for OIDC sessions.
